stage1_ideas.raw_text:
Ticker: AAL
Company: American Airlines Group Inc.
Direction: Bearish
Live / Emerging Catalyst: U.S. bombing of Kharg Island military targets Friday evening, with Trump threatening to strike oil infrastructure if Iran continues blocking the Strait of Hormuz. Millions of barrels of oil remain trapped in the Persian Gulf with Strait traffic effectively at a standstill, and Iran's new supreme leader vowed to keep the Strait closed. Jet fuel prices have doubled since the conflict, far exceeding the roughly one-third rise in crude oil prices.
Catalyst Status: Active
Catalyst Event Date: Ongoing since Feb 28; Kharg Island strike announced post-market Mar 13
Market Visibility Date: Kharg Island bombing — Friday evening Mar 13 (after market close); crack spread blowout — Mar 4-12 via Reuters
Hard-Date Status: No Hard Date
Next Earnings Date: Data unavailable — verify in R2
Source quality: Mixed (primary government/IEA sources for oil and conflict data; secondary reporting for airline-specific financials)
Why this company is the cleanest expression: United Airlines and Carnival Corp entered 2026 with 0% of their fuel requirements hedged, leaving them completely exposed. United Airlines has disclosed that every $1 change in the price of a barrel of fuel impacts its annual expenses by approximately $116 million. AAL shares this 0% hedging posture. An analyst noted downside risk to estimates as higher jet fuel prices are incorporated, stating "we see most risk for American and now forecast negative EPS this year," downgrading AAL from buy to neutral — and that call was made at ~$77 oil, not today's ~$99. AAL's weaker balance sheet versus peers creates maximum operating leverage to sustained fuel cost increases. The widening gap between crude oil and refined jet fuel costs has created intense pressure on airline margins, and most hedging programs are tied to crude oil benchmarks rather than the refined fuel airlines actually consume. Even partially hedged peers are exposed; AAL's 0% hedging makes it the purest bearish expression.
What already moved: The price of Brent crude has gained more than 40 percent since the war began in late February. Jet fuel prices have doubled since the Iran conflict, far outpacing a one-third rise in crude prices. In Asia, jet fuel refining margins widened from about $21/bbl pre-conflict to as much as $144, remaining unusually high at $65 as of Wednesday.
What has already repriced in this stock (and approximate magnitude): American Airlines and JetBlue saw double-digit percentage drops as their unhedged positions left them equally exposed to the spot market. UAL's share price cratered by 33% on March 12 alone. AAL has experienced comparable severe selling since late February.
What has not repriced enough: (1) The Kharg Island bombing was announced Friday evening after market close — Trump on Friday evening announced he'd ordered strikes on Kharg Island, which is an unpriced Monday gap event. (2) The analyst downgrade to negative EPS was at ~$77 oil; at $99 WTI the negative EPS is materially worse and consensus has not caught up. (3) The mismatch between crude oil hedging and jet fuel prices becomes particularly problematic during geopolitical crises that disrupt refining capacity. AAL with 0% hedging of any kind faces the full brunt of the crack spread blowout — an incremental headwind beyond crude-focused models. (4) Prediction markets assign only a 19.5% probability to the Iran conflict resolving by March 31, suggesting duration risk is higher than many models assume.
Expected tactical payoff range: 8–15% additional downside over 1–2 weeks, assuming oil sustains above $95 and no ceasefire
Thesis: AAL is the most fuel-exposed major U.S. airline — 0% hedged with the weakest balance sheet among the big three. The Friday evening Kharg Island escalation creates an unpriced Monday catalyst. Jet fuel doubling versus crude's ~40% rise means AAL's true cost hit is far worse than headline oil suggests, and the Street's downward earnings revision cycle — initiated at $77 oil — has not caught up to $99+ reality.
Transmission type and why lag exists: First-order (oil/jet fuel → airline fuel cost → EPS destruction). The primary lag is the post-close Kharg Island escalation creating an immediate Monday repricing event. The secondary lag is that sell-side consensus has not yet revised to sustained $99+ oil, and the crack spread amplification is structurally undermodeled in most airline coverage.
Why the market may still be late: An analyst already forecast negative EPS when oil was at ~$77. Oil is now $99 with Brent at $103. The earnings impact at current levels is roughly 30% worse than the initial downgrade scenario, yet consensus has not been fully revised. The crack spread angle means the true fuel cost is even worse than the crude-implied estimate. The post-close Kharg Island escalation has not been priced.
Best reason this may already be priced: Airline stocks have already been decimated — UAL down 33% in a single day, AAL in double-digit decline territory. Peak panic may already be reflected. White House officials have said they expect oil prices to fall dramatically once Operation Epic Fury comes to an end. Any ceasefire signal or Strait reopening would trigger a violent short squeeze. The 400-million-barrel IEA reserve release and U.S. easing of Russian oil sanctions are designed to cap prices.
Evidence of delayed or incomplete pricing: (1) Kharg Island bombing post-close Friday — not yet reflected in AAL stock. (2) Analyst negative EPS forecast at $77 oil vs. current $99 — material revision gap. (3) Jet fuel doubled vs. crude up ~40% — incremental damage beyond crude-focused models. (4) Sub-20% conflict resolution probability by March 31 implies duration risk the market may underweight.
Extension / continuation read: Stock already down heavily from pre-war levels. However, the Kharg escalation is a fresh catalyst with no prior pricing, and the crack spread dimension adds an underappreciated layer. The bear thesis is partially priced but not exhausted given continued war escalation and low resolution probability.
Options / OI / dark-pool read: Data unavailable — verify in R2
Historical transmission check: During the 2022 Russia-Ukraine oil spike (Brent above $120), U.S. airline stocks experienced 30–50% drawdowns over several weeks. The current situation is arguably more severe for unhedged carriers because of the unprecedented crack spread blowout and 0% hedging posture. Historically, fuel price spikes during Middle Eastern conflicts have remained elevated for months.
Practical tradability: Adequate (AAL is liquid with high daily volume; however, borrow availability for short positions and extreme IV on puts must be verified in R2)
Expected repricing window: Monday March 16 open + 1–2 weeks
Why this is actionable now: The Kharg Island post-close escalation creates a specific Monday repricing catalyst. Oil above $100, crack spread at extreme levels, 0% hedging, and sub-20% resolution probability by month-end collectively argue the bearish revision cycle continues.
Main invalidation: Ceasefire or Strait reopening announcement over the weekend. Oil falling below $85 on SPR release, diplomatic breakthrough, or Russian supply normalization. Government airline support or bailout intervention.
Crowding risk: Medium (airline-bearish trade is a common expression of the oil shock, but the crack spread amplification angle and post-close Kharg timing provide differentiation; the trade is not as crowded as the direct oil-long expression)
Confidence: Low (significant repricing has already occurred; the remaining gap depends on duration risk and Monday-specific gap from Kharg Island escalation; elevated IV may limit practical returns on options)
Key uncertainties for R2 verification: Exact AAL stock decline from pre-war levels to quantify remaining gap. Current borrow availability and short interest. Implied volatility levels on puts and put spread pricing. Q1 2026 earnings date. Updated sell-side consensus EPS at current oil levels. Whether AAL has added any hedging since the war began. Balance sheet liquidity runway at $99+ oil for extended period.
Known idiosyncratic risks: AAL's weaker balance sheet creates both opportunity (maximum earnings leverage) and unpredictable risk (potential credit events, government intervention, restructuring speculation). Binary ceasefire risk could cause violent squeeze. Extremely elevated IV makes options expensive and may erode returns even on a correct directional call.
Source support caveat: AAL's 0% hedging posture and the negative EPS forecast are sourced from secondary financial reporting citing analyst notes and regulatory filings. Kharg Island timing confirmed from multiple primary sources (White House, CNBC, Foreign Policy, Axios). Crack spread data from Reuters/BofA. Some specific AAL figures require verification against primary company filings in R2.

flow_details.raw_output:
---

## AAL Bearish Thesis — Flow & Dark-Pool Tape Read

### Catalyst Context

After President Trump issued a vague new threat, saying "watch what happens" to Iran's leaders on Friday, Mr. Trump announced later in the day that the U.S. had bombed Kharg Island, a vital oil hub that serves as Iran's main oil export terminal. Iran's attacks continue to gridlock shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has said the U.S. Navy could start escorting tankers through the strategic waterway as oil prices remain over $100 per barrel. On Friday, Brent crude oil futures closed above $100 per barrel for the second straight day, and the global oil benchmark's price has surged more than 40% since the Iran war started.

As of Mar 13, 2026, American Airlines (AAL) is trading at a price of 10.55 USD, with a previous close of 11.04 USD. American Airlines continues to battle through a tough month, with its stock down 27%. CNBC reported "Biggest U.S. Airlines to Absorb $11B from Jet Fuel Shockwave" and most major U.S. airlines have distanced themselves from fuel hedging programs over the last few years, meaning a prolonged rise in crude oil prices could really weigh on AAL's financials.

The catalyst is live, escalating, and directly negative for airlines: oil above $100, Strait of Hormuz effectively closed, and the latest Kharg Island strikes introduce further escalation risk. AAL is unhedged against fuel costs.

---

### OPTIONS FLOW INTERPRETATION

**1. AAL260417P00010000 — PUT $10.0, exp 04/17/2026 — BUILDING (NEW) — ASK-HEAVY**

This is the single most important line in the dataset. OI went from 515 on 03/02 to 87,015 on 03/13 — a massive, multi-session build over ~10 days. The lifecycle is textbook fresh opening accumulation:

- **03/03:** 61,882 volume vs. 1,092 prior OI; 59,758 on ask-side (~97%) — overwhelmingly aggressive buying. Premium: ~$1.85M.
- **03/04:** 24,468 volume, 24,228 ask-side (~99%). OI jumped to 61,324, confirming the prior day's volume was fresh opening.
- **03/05:** 34,809 volume, 30,325 ask-side (~87%). OI rose to 81,505 — sustained new opening.
- **03/06:** Ask-heavy again (5,235 ask vs 905 bid). OI climbed to 106,754.
- **03/09:** Massive 81,261 contracts but heavily bid-side (31,774 bid vs. 6,181 ask). OI rose to 111,678 but then *dropped* from 111,678 → 83,644 over the next session. This suggests a significant partial exit or profit-taking event around 03/09–03/10 after the initial war escalation moved price.
- **03/10–03/13:** OI stabilized at ~83,600–87,000 with moderate daily volume, mixed sides. The position was partially closed/rolled but a large ~87K OI base remains.

**Interpretation:** A very large put position was built aggressively at ask over multiple sessions (03/03–03/06) before the catalyst escalated. Around ~25K contracts were likely closed near 03/09–03/10, representing profit-taking or a partial roll after the initial sharp move. The remaining ~87K OI is substantial and sustained. The 04/17 expiry brackets the ongoing war and the 04/23 earnings date. The $10 strike is now close to ATM with spot at $10.55, giving this position significant delta. **Strongly bearish.**

**2. AAL260515P00010000 — PUT $10.0, exp 05/15/2026 — OI: BUILDING (+391%) — ASK-HEAVY**

OI rose from 3,687 → 18,305 over the period. The build shows clear multi-session accumulation:

- **03/03–03/06:** Ask-heavy accumulation on 03/05 (3,925 ask vs. 157 bid; OI rose 5,319→8,472) and 03/06 (2,387 ask vs. 188 bid; OI rose to ~11,300 area implied).
- **03/09:** 4,398 contracts, relatively balanced, but OI rose to 15,060 next day — confirming opening.
- **03/10:** 3,751 contracts, heavily bid-side (3,630 bid) — likely partial close / profit-taking as OI rose to 15,060 but then jumped to 18,227, suggesting mixed behavior.
- **03/13:** 6,603 volume with 5,640 ask-side (85%+ ask) — aggressive new buying, premium $604K. Last price at $0.95 with rising IV (~64%).

**Interpretation:** Sustained multi-session put build with strong ask-side dominance. The 05/15 expiry extends well past the Q1 earnings date (04/23) and covers the full war uncertainty window. The most recent session (03/13) showed the most aggressive ask-side buying of the entire period. **Strongly bearish, fresh and accelerating.**

**3. AAL260320P00011000 — PUT $11.0, exp 03/20/2026 — OI: BUILDING (+68%) — BALANCED**

OI rose from ~27,240 to ~43,713. This is a weekly expiry put, now ~$0.45 ITM with spot at $10.55.

- **03/06:** 11,927 contracts, 7,750 ask vs. 3,303 bid — ask-heavy, bullish on the put.
- **03/09:** 22,728 contracts, but 16,528 bid vs. 5,335 ask — heavily bid-side (likely closing or rolling for profit after the initial plunge).
- **03/10:** 5,266 contracts, 4,345 ask — OI jumped from 26,445 to 41,415 after the prior session's mixed activity. Fresh opening confirmed.
- **03/11:** 13,095 contracts, 11,977 bid — heavy bid-side, while OI *dropped* from 41,415 → 31,158. This is clear closing/profit-taking.
- **03/12–03/13:** OI rebuilt to 43,713 with ask-side participation on 03/12.

**Interpretation:** This line shows a pattern of build → partial close → rebuild. The ITM position is growing again but the balanced / choppy side profile and the 03/20 expiry (one week away) make this more likely expiry-driven hedging / active trading. Supportive of bearish sentiment but less clean than the April/May lines. Nearing expiry-week — mild pin/hedging distortion risk.

**4. AAL260320C00015000 — CALL $15.0, exp 03/20/2026 — OI: BUILDING (+311%) — BALANCED**

OI is large (~71,500+) but this is a $15 call with spot at $10.55 — ~42% OTM, trading at $0.01, with one week to expiry. This is almost certainly worthless and the heavy bid-side flow on the largest volume days (03/03: 26,975 bid; 03/05: 32,606 bid) suggests sellers collecting premium. The OI stability and lack of follow-through volume in recent days confirms this is written/sold premium, not speculative buying. **This is not a bullish counter-signal.** The OI was likely established earlier and is simply expiring. Neutral to mildly bearish (sellers collecting premium from speculative call buyers).

**Aggregate Options Composition:**
- 52% puts / 48% calls, with the call/put volume ratio declining to 0.5 on the most recent session — a clear bearish shift.
- Ask-side share has accelerated to 63% on the most recent session, the highest in the 15-session window — indicating accumulating urgency.
- 24 sweeps across the period indicate some execution urgency across multiple prints.
- Largest single alerts include a $0.9M PUT $10 04/17 floor trade and a $0.7M PUT $8 01/15/2027 — deep bearish positioning across multiple timeframes.

---

### DARK POOL INTERPRETATION

- Total notional: $124.8M across 10 sessions / 500 prints.
- Price clustering: 78% of prints in $10.98–$12.55 — this defines the structural zone.
- Repeated levels: **$12.46 (31x), $10.55 (29x), $11.79 (24x).**
- Current price (03/13 close): **$10.55** — sitting *exactly* on the second-heaviest dark-pool level.

**Key structural read:**
- **$12.46 (31 prints):** This is the most concentrated level and sits well above current price. It likely marks institutional distribution that occurred earlier in the selloff. Price has broken well below this zone and has not recovered — **bearish confirmation.**
- **$11.79 (24 prints):** Intermediate level, also broken to the downside — further bearish confirmation.
- **$10.55 (29 prints):** Current price is sitting exactly on this level. This is a critical inflection point — it could act as temporary support, but a clean break below would accelerate the move toward the 52-week low of $8.50.

The 03/10 session showed a massive $27.7M dark-pool notional day — the highest of the 10-session window — coinciding with AAL's -2.88% drop to $11.11. This elevated activity during a down-move leans distributive.

**Dark-pool verdict:** Price has broken below the two heaviest structural levels ($12.46 and $11.79) and is now sitting on the last major cluster at $10.55. The weight of distribution is overhead. A sustained break below $10.55 would be a clean bearish structural breakdown with no obvious support until ~$8.50 (52-week low).

---

### SYNTHESIS

**Options read:**
Multi-session, multi-expiry put accumulation is the dominant signal. The AAL260417P00010000 ($10 put, 04/17) shows a massive 87K OI build from near-zero, accumulated heavily at ask over 4+ consecutive sessions — one of the cleanest directional builds in this dataset. The AAL260515P00010000 ($10 put, 05/15) shows a +391% OI build with ask-heavy execution accelerating into the most recent session. The call/put ratio has shifted to 0.5 (bearish) and ask-side accumulation has accelerated to 63%. The only notable call OI (the $15 03/20 line) is a dead-money position that appears to be sold premium. IV is elevated (~64–68% on the $10 puts) but not in the extreme 80–90 IV-rank zone that would require automatic downgrade, and the multi-session OI build and immediate catalyst alignment are unusually strong. **Strongly bearish.**

**Dark-pool read:**
Price has broken below the $12.46 and $11.79 structural levels and now sits exactly on the $10.55 level — the final major cluster. Overhead distribution is heavy. Biggest notional day ($27.7M on 03/10) came during a down-session. A sustained break below $10.55 would open significant downside. **Bearish, with the $10.55 level as the immediate structural pivot to monitor.**

**Dealer gamma / timing context:**
Data unavailable — verify in R2. The elevated volume (108% above 3-month average on 03/10) and accelerating put accumulation suggest a negative-gamma environment is plausible, which would amplify downside moves. The Friday evening Kharg Island catalyst occurred after the close, meaning the next session's opening flow will be critical. Given that the flow build occurred *before* this latest escalation, the positioning is front-running further downside that the new catalyst may now deliver.

**Downgrade factors:**
- IV is elevated (~64–68%), though not in the extreme zone requiring mandatory downgrade.
- Broader airline sector is under macro pressure (DAL -2.16%, UAL -3.68% on 03/10) — some of this flow is sector-wide rather than purely idiosyncratic. However, AAL's lack of fuel hedging and high leverage make it the most exposed name.
- The AAL260320P00011000 line shows churn (build/close/rebuild) rather than clean one-direction accumulation.
- The 03/20 put is within the expiry-week window, introducing mild pin/hedging distortion risk on that specific line (but the 04/17 and 05/15 lines are well outside this window).
- The partial ~25K close on the 04/17 line around 03/09–03/10 suggests some early profit-taking, though the remaining 87K base is still massive.
- Dealer gamma data unavailable.

---

**Options read:** Strongly bearish — massive multi-session put OI build across April and May expiries, ask-heavy accumulation, declining call/put ratio, catalyst-aligned strike selection, and fresh aggressive buying on the most recent session.

**Dark-pool read:** Bearish — price has broken below two major structural levels, sitting on the last support at $10.55, with overhead distribution weight.

**Dealer gamma / timing context:** Data unavailable — verify in R2. Catalyst occurred post-market Friday; positioning was built ahead of this escalation.

**Downgrade factors:** Elevated IV (moderate, not extreme); some sector-wide macro flow reducing idiosyncratic signal; partial profit-taking on the largest line (~25K closed); one tracked contract shows churn; dealer gamma unavailable; 03/20 expiry line subject to mild pin risk.

## **[SUPPORTIVE]**

The tape strongly supports the bearish thesis for AAL. The options flow shows one of the cleanest multi-session put builds I can identify in this dataset — an ~87K OI position at the $10 strike for April, accumulated aggressively at ask from near-zero over 4+ sessions, with a parallel +391% build in May $10 puts that is *still accelerating*. Dark-pool structure confirms price has broken below major institutional levels. The catalyst — oil above $100, Strait of Hormuz blockade, and now Kharg Island strikes with threats to hit oil infrastructure — directly impairs AAL's unhedged cost structure. The key level to watch is the $10.55 dark-pool cluster where price sits now; a sustained break below this on Monday's open would represent a clean structural breakdown with the options positioning already in place to benefit.

confirmation_details.kill_reason: None. Moderate disagreement noted: options positioning data is pre-Kharg and shows churn rather than clean build, while cross-asset and prediction markets are strongly aligned. The options data is ~1 trading day stale relative to the Kharg escalation (no penalty applied, but the Friday post-close ev
confirmation_details.raw_output:
CATALYST CONFIRMATION
Ticker: AAL
Direction: Bearish
Primary catalyst: Iran war escalation — Kharg Island bombing Friday evening post-close, Strait of Hormuz closure, jet fuel price doubling, AAL 0% fuel hedged with weakest balance sheet among Big Three
Catalyst profile: War-driven energy shock → refined fuel cost pass-through → unhedged airline margin destruction
Catalyst proxy basket: WTI crude, Brent crude, RBOB gasoline, jet fuel crack spreads, DAL, UAL, LUV (inverse), defense/shipping names (directional)
Dim 1 Score: 68
Dim 2 Score: 78
Dim 3 Score: 88
Weighting basis: Fast catalyst (active war, headline-driven), time-to-resolution unknown but near-term event window (Monday open gap). Default fast-catalyst weighting: Options/positioning 45%, Prediction markets 25%, Cross-asset 30%.
Primary CDS: 76
Disagreement flag: MODERATE DISAGREEMENT
Collision check: Competing catalyst identified — Trump signaling Strait reopening via Navy escorts, and Iran reportedly considering allowing yuan-denominated tanker transits. Opposition Weight: 0.2. Competing CDS estimated at 30 (early-stage, vague, no operational detail yet). Effective CDS adjustment: 76 − (30 × 0.2) = 70.
Effective CDS: 70
Catalyst health state: STABLE

Dimension 1 — Options / positioning:
The provided flow module data rates the tape as SUPPORTIVE but flags meaningful noise. IV is elevated at 64–68%, indicating the market has already priced significant vol into AAL options — this is not a sleepy name being surprised. The AAL 03/20 put line shows churn (build/close/rebuild) rather than clean directional accumulation, which is a weaker signal than a fresh sustained build. Broader airline sector saw correlated weakness (DAL -2.16%, UAL -3.68% on 03/10), confirming sector-wide pressure rather than purely idiosyncratic flow. The expiry-week proximity of the 03/20 put introduces gamma-decay drag. Net assessment: flow is directionally supportive of the bearish thesis but is noisy, churning, and partially reflects known sector macro rather than fresh catalyst conviction from the Friday Kharg escalation (which occurred after the last flow snapshot). Score reflects supportive but imperfect positioning, with a caution that the post-close Kharg event is not yet embedded in any options data.

Dimension 2 — Prediction markets:
With $4.5 million traded on the "Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?" market, this is a well-trafficked contract. The leading "December 31" outcome is priced at 84%, indicating traders collectively believe there is roughly an 84% chance the conflict ends by year-end — but critically, the ORCA idea card cites only a 19.5% probability of conflict resolution by March 31. That implies ~80% probability the conflict persists through the full repricing window identified in the trade. The U.S. invasion of Iran by March 31 is priced at only 11%, suggesting the market expects sustained air/sea operations without ground escalation — a scenario that keeps the Strait closed via drone harassment without triggering a resolution-forcing ground campaign. Iran's new supreme leader explicitly vowed continuation: Mojtaba Khamenei said the closure of the Strait of Hormuz should be continued as a "tool to pressure the enemy." RBC Capital Markets noted "there is significant skepticism that a robust US Navy tanker escort service will be operational soon." Prediction-market odds strongly support the catalyst's duration — the war continues, the Strait stays closed, jet fuel stays elevated through the trade's 1–2 week window.

Dimension 3 — Cross-asset:
The cross-asset complex is the strongest confirmation dimension. Brent crude oil futures closed above $100 per barrel for the second straight day. U.S. crude oil gained 3.11%, or $2.98, to settle at $98.71 per barrel. Brent crude has gained more than 40% since the war began in late February. Jet fuel has jumped to more than $4 a gallon after spending most of 2025 near $2. The refined product crack spread blowout is the critical amplifier: Jet fuel costs have rocketed from their previous $85–90 baseline to peaks approaching $200 per barrel, based on Air New Zealand's reporting. The Strait remains effectively shut: The Strait of Hormuz has been effectively closed since the U.S. and Israel began the war on Iran. The capacity of bypass pipelines is unable to match the amount of oil shipped through the strait, with a deficit of about 12 million barrels per day. The Friday Kharg Island escalation adds a fresh accelerant: Trump on Friday night announced the U.S. had bombed Kharg Island, targeting Iran's most critical oil terminal. The assault on military sites on Kharg Island but not the energy facilities amounts to a warning shot and a threat that the US may strike targets that are part of the country's energy infrastructure. This is a direct threat of further oil supply destruction. AAL-specific confirmation: Each additional penny per gallon translates to approximately $50 million in added annual fuel expenses for American. Every 10-cent increase in fuel costs can move American's earnings per share by roughly 25%. American Airlines delivered an adjusted pre-tax profit of merely $352 million for 2025, while Delta achieved $5 billion and United reached $4.6 billion. TD Cowen cut its price target from $17 to $13; Rothschild downgraded AAL from Buy to Neutral and cut its target from $17 to $12.50, citing "limited financial flexibility in a higher-cost environment." These downgrades occurred at lower oil prices than the current ~$99 WTI. The cross-asset complex — oil, refined products, crack spreads, shipping disruption, airline peer underperformance — is fully aligned and accelerating into the weekend.

Why this matters for the trade:
The Kharg Island bombing is a post-close Friday event that has not been priced into AAL equity. Trump threatened to attack the oil infrastructure if Tehran continues blocking ships from traversing the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's new supreme leader said the Strait must stay closed and all U.S. military bases in the Middle East should shut immediately, warning of further attacks. This is an escalatory stalemate, not a de-escalation path. The catalyst's transmission mechanism — Strait closure → oil spike → jet fuel crack spread blowout → unhedged airline margin destruction — is intact and strengthening. AAL's $36.5B debt load, 0% hedging, $352M pre-tax profit (vs. peers at $4.6–5.0B), and analyst downgrades-at-lower-oil all confirm it remains the cleanest bearish expression. The gap between the Friday escalation and Monday's open is the specific event the ORCA idea targets, and the catalyst evidence supports that gap risk.

Recommended action: CONFIRM

Primary downgrade / kill reason: None. Moderate disagreement noted: options positioning data is pre-Kharg and shows churn rather than clean build, while cross-asset and prediction markets are strongly aligned. The options data is ~1 trading day stale relative to the Kharg escalation (no penalty applied, but the Friday post-close event means Monday's flow data will be the true test). The competing catalyst (Strait reopening via escorts or yuan-denominated transit) is embryonic — Hegseth said only that "the only thing prohibiting transit in the straits right now is Iran shooting at shipping" — with no operational timeline, and Energy Secretary Chris Wright stated the U.S. is "not ready" to escort tankers through the Strait yet. This is not strong enough to kill or downgrade.

Key missing checks for R2: (1) Monday pre-market AAL options flow and OI rebuild post-Kharg — confirm fresh put builds, not just churn. (2) Monday pre-market oil and jet fuel futures gap — quantify the overnight repricing. (3) Updated Polymarket ceasefire-by-March-31 probability — check for weekend movement. (4) AAL-specific dark pool prints Monday AM. (5) Whether AAL management comments at the J.P. Morgan Industrials Conference on March 17 provide any hedging or guidance update. (6) Any weekend diplomatic developments or SPR release announcements that could compress oil. (7) Actual Monday IV levels in AAL — if IV gaps above 75–80%, the practical return on puts may be materially impaired even if direction is correct.

stage1_ideas.raw_text:
Ticker: SOC
Company: Sable Offshore Corp.
Direction: Bullish
Live / Emerging Catalyst: Trump administration federal directive to restore the Santa Ynez oil unit.
Catalyst Status: Recently Triggered
Catalyst Event Date: 2026-03-13
Market Visibility Date: 2026-03-13
Hard-Date Status: No Hard Date
Next Earnings Date: Data unavailable — verify in R2
Source quality: Secondary
Why this company is the cleanest expression: SOC is effectively a single-asset entity formed specifically to acquire and restart the Santa Ynez Unit (SYU) offshore California. The federal directive directly targets their sole operational bottleneck and existential regulatory overhang.
What already moved: Global crude oil has spiked violently (driven by the US bombing of Kharg Island and Strait of Hormuz threats), and the broader equity market has liquidated (S&P 500 hitting new lows for the year).
What has already repriced in this stock (and approximate magnitude): Data unavailable — verify in R2
What has not repriced enough: The compounding valuation effect of a massive idiosyncratic regulatory unlock occurring simultaneously with a generational spike in the underlying commodity (oil), which is likely being masked by broad index liquidation.
Expected tactical payoff range (% move): 20-40%
Thesis (2–4 sentences max): The Trump administration has officially directed Sable Offshore to restore the Santa Ynez oil unit, directly neutralizing the company's primary regulatory overhang. This idiosyncratic silver-bullet catalyst is hitting the tape on the exact same day that global oil prices are surging due to US strikes on Iran's Kharg Island. Because the broader equity market is in a severe liquidation phase, SOC's massive fundamental unlock is highly likely to be lost in the noise or sold down with the market, creating a severely mispriced entry for a company that just won its regulatory battle alongside a massive commodity tailwind.
Transmission type and why lag exists: First-Order. Lag exists because the news is breaking during a severe macro liquidation event, causing idiosyncratic positive news to be overshadowed by broad de-risking, index selling, and geopolitical panic.
Why the market may still be late: Retail and institutional bandwidth is entirely consumed by the Iran war, Strait of Hormuz threats, and the resulting broad market selloff.
Best reason this may already be priced: The market may have aggressively bid SOC up in the pre-market immediately upon the headline crossing, or investors may heavily discount the federal directive's legal ability to quickly override California state law.
Evidence of delayed or incomplete pricing: 1) The catalyst dropped on a Friday amidst a headline-dominating macro panic and S&P 500 new lows, a classic structural environment for idiosyncratic news to be ignored or sold into by index funds. 2) The simultaneous spike in oil prices provides a secondary valuation tailwind that compounds the regulatory win, a dual-factor shock that is rarely priced efficiently in a single panic-driven session.
Extension / continuation read: Data unavailable — verify in R2
Options / OI / dark-pool read: Data unavailable — verify in R2
Historical transmission check: Data unavailable — verify in R2
Public positioning confirmation (optional): Data unavailable — verify in R2
Practical tradability: Adequate
Expected repricing window: 1 to 3 weeks
Why this is actionable now: The convergence of a binary regulatory win and a massive macro commodity tailwind during a broad market panic creates a rare dislocation and entry opportunity.
Main invalidation: California state regulators immediately file an injunction that successfully blocks the federal directive, trapping SOC in prolonged litigation despite the administration's order.
Crowding risk: Low
Confidence: Medium
Key uncertainties for R2 verification: Exact intraday price response of SOC to the headline; legal viability and timeline of the federal directive vs. California Coastal Commission jurisdiction.
Known idiosyncratic risks: Single-asset concentration risk; heavy debt load typical of SPAC E&P acquisitions; state vs. federal legal jurisdiction battles.
Source support caveat: Relies on CNBC headline "Trump administration directs Sable Offshore to restore Santa Ynez oil unit."

flow_details.raw_output:
Now let me synthesize all the data.

---

## SOC — Flow & Dark-Pool Tape Interpretation (v5.7)

### Catalyst Context

The Trump administration on Friday directed Sable Offshore to restore oil drilling operations off the southern California coast. A directive from Energy Secretary Chris Wright following a Trump executive order cited "supply disruption risks" as reasons for invoking the Defense Production Act. Sable has stated that it can produce 45,000-60,000 barrels of oil a day if the Santa Ynez Unit were to restart.

The catalyst is live and real — the formal DPA directive dropped today (3/13). SOC stock jumped this week after reports that President Trump plans to invoke emergency powers to support the company's California offshore oil project. The stock price gained 5.25% on the last trading day (Thursday, 12th Mar 2026), rising from $16.58 to $17.45. The price has risen in 9 of the last 10 days and is up by 120.61% over the past 2 weeks.

Current price: ~$16.79 (3/13). Important: In attempting to restart the defective onshore pipelines, Sable has run into widespread public opposition and accumulated a series of violations and criminal charges. To date, Sable has still not received all necessary approvals for a restart from California state agencies. Litigation and state opposition remain material headline risk.

---

### OPTIONS FLOW — DETAILED ANALYSIS

**Multi-day alert composition:** 18 sessions, 114 alerts; 88% calls. Composition is overwhelmingly bullish by alert type. 22 sweeps, 0 blocks — execution style is sweep-heavy, suggesting urgency. However, **59% bid-side** across alerts represents a distribution bias, which is at odds with the overwhelmingly call-heavy composition.

**Daily volume trend:**
- Call volume over 5 sessions: 11,626 → 6,525 → 38,645 → 19,782 → 31,805 — a clear spike starting 3/11 coinciding with the Bloomberg report. The call/put ratio oscillated from 4.5 to as low as 1.5 on 3/12 before recovering to 4.0 on 3/13.
- Ask-side share: 49% → 51% → 43% → 62% → 57%. The increase from 43% to 62% on 3/12 then 57% on 3/13 suggests accumulation firmed after the initial event-driven chaos on 3/11.
- Net premium positive 4 of 5 sessions — broadly consistent with bullish positioning.

**Contract-level analysis:**

1. **SOC260313C00020000 (Call $20 exp 3/13, 0DTE today):** Built from 0 OI to 1,180 on the final day. On 3/13 (expiry), volume 6,283 vs OI 1,180 with last price $0.03 — far OTM, expiring worthless. IV at 77.93 (absurdly high expiry-day artifact). Ask-heavy on 3/13 but this is lottery/expiry churn — **signal value: negligible.**

2. **SOC260320C00020000 (Call $20 exp 3/20):** OI peaked at ~14,055 on 3/11, the big event day. Since then OI has **declined** to 12,079 (−12% from peak). On 3/11, bid_volume (4,643) was nearly 3x ask_volume (1,669) — this was overwhelmingly bid-side, suggesting selling/closing rather than buying. On 3/12 and 3/13, ask-side exceeded bid-side, but volumes were smaller (2,753 and 2,426). Overall flagged as BID-HEAVY and declining OI → this is a **net unwind** of the $20 strike position post-spike. The massive 3/11 print looks like a profit-take or close against an existing large position (OI was already ~13,800 before the event).

3. **SOC260320C00015000 (Call $15 exp 3/20):** OI rose from 9,241 → 12,570 (+36%), the only line showing material OI growth. OI built steadily: 9,241 → 9,562 → 11,362 → 10,760 → 10,505 → 12,469 → 12,579 → 12,570. This is the most constructive line. On 3/10, heavy volume (3,175) with ask_volume (1,804) > bid_volume (1,081) — ask-heavy, suggesting buying. On 3/05, also heavy volume but bid-heavy (1,735 vs 1,084). Side balance is mixed overall, flagged as BALANCED. This $15 strike is now ITM with stock at ~$17, so it functions partly as stock replacement / deep ITM positioning rather than convex upside speculation. The +36% OI build across multiple sessions is the strongest multi-session build in the dataset and aligns with the catalyst window (3/20 expiry).

4. **SOC260313C00018000 (Call $18 exp 3/13, 0DTE today):** OI peaked at 672 on 3/12 and fell to 561 on 3/13 (expiry day). On 3/13, volume 1,903 with bid_volume (1,163) > ask_volume (581) — overwhelmingly bid-side, i.e., sellers closing on expiry. Last price $0.02. This is pure expiry-related closing flow — **signal value: negligible.**

**Overall contract picture per the summary:** 0/6 rising OI, 1/6 weakening/unwind, 3/6 bid-heavy, overall described as **"net unwind — positioning weakening."** This is a critical red flag.

**Unusual activity assessment:** The activity is clearly unusual in absolute terms — volume spikes of 2.7x, $4.9M total premium, catalyst-aligned timing, sweep-heavy execution. However, the unusualness appears driven primarily by the 3/11 news shock, and the subsequent OI trajectory shows unwind rather than sustained build. The one exception is the $15 call line.

---

### DARK POOL — DETAILED ANALYSIS

**Structure:** 99 prints over 3 sessions, $35.3M total notional. Daily ramp: $1.9M → $21.5M → $11.9M. The massive spike on 3/11 ($21.5M) coincides with the Bloomberg headline and circuit-breaker halt day.

**Price clustering:** 84% of prints in $15.82–$17.80 range, consistent with the trading range since the breakout. Repeated levels: $17.45 (5x), $16.81 (5x), $17.07 (4x).

- $17.45 is precisely the 3/12 close and the day's high — a cluster at the high of the move could suggest distribution (large holders selling into strength) or pivot formation.
- $16.81 is mid-range, a secondary structural level.
- $17.07 is mid-range.

**Current price context:** Stock is at ~$16.79 as of 3/13, which is **below** the $17.45 cluster level and right at the $16.81 cluster. The stock pulled back from $17.50 to $16.79 on 3/13, so it is now trading at/below the densest dark-pool structural zone rather than above it.

**LIT flow:** $0.8M over 2 sessions, 88% sell-side — this is a small sample but shows supply, consistent with distribution.

---

### SYNTHESIS

**Options read:**
Call-heavy composition and ask-side accumulation trend are nominally bullish. However, the contract-level detail tells a very different story: the overall position picture is "net unwind — positioning weakening" with 0/6 contracts showing rising OI. The only constructive line is the $15 call with +36% OI build, but this is now deep ITM and side balance is mixed. The largest-premium lines ($20 calls) show peak OI on 3/11 followed by steady decline — classic profit-taking / closing behavior post-event spike. Bid-heavy distribution across 3/6 tracked contracts and 59% bid-side across alerts reinforce this read. The 0DTE expiry lines are pure noise. The multi-session build that would be required for a [SUPPORTIVE] classification exists only on one line ($15 strike) out of six.

**Dark-pool read:**
Dense clustering in $15.82–$17.80 with key level at $17.45. Stock is now at $16.79, sitting **below** the $17.45 level and at/near the $16.81 level. This is ambiguous-to-slightly-bearish: the stock has not held above the primary dark-pool cluster. The massive 3/11 print day followed by price rejection from $17.50 back to $16.79 leans toward distribution rather than accumulation. Lit flow (88% sell-side) reinforces supply at these levels.

**Dealer gamma / timing context:**
Data unavailable — verify in R2. The 3/11 flow occurred during a circuit-breaker halt/resume session, and much of the heaviest volume was clearly event-reactive rather than sustained conviction. Volume fell on 3/12 (-10M shares per stockinvest.us) even as price rose, creating a volume/price divergence. The catalyst formally arrived today (3/13 Friday morning), so the tape has not yet fully priced in the actual directive vs. the prior Bloomberg rumor.

**Downgrade factors:**
- Overall contract picture is **net unwind**, with 0/6 contracts showing rising OI
- 3/6 contracts are bid-heavy (distribution)
- The largest OI builds ($20 strike) peaked on 3/11 and have been declining — closing/profit-taking, not fresh conviction
- 59% bid-side across multi-day alerts
- Stock is below the primary $17.45 dark-pool cluster level as of 3/13
- Lit flow 88% sell-side
- 2 of 4 detailed contracts are 0DTE (3/13 expiry) with negligible remaining signal value
- Elevated IV across all lines (most >100% IV) without unusually strong multi-session OI confirmation
- The formal DPA directive dropped today (3/13), creating a potential "buy the rumor, sell the news" dynamic — flow data may reflect the rumor phase and not the news phase
- Volume/price divergence on 3/12
- Significant litigation and state opposition remain as material headline risk factors, including civil charges brought by the California Attorney General and criminal charges brought by the Santa Barbara County District Attorney's Office
- OI lifecycle on the key $20 strike line suggests likely exit/unwind rather than fresh opening

---

## Final Tape Read

**Options read:** Nominally call-heavy but contract-level OI is in net unwind; only one of six lines shows constructive build ($15 ITM call, +36%). Bid-heavy distribution dominates. Post-event profit-taking is the primary lifecycle read.

**Dark-pool read:** Dense structural zone at $15.82–$17.80 with key level $17.45. Stock at $16.79 is below this level and near the secondary $16.81 cluster. Ambiguous leaning slightly bearish; 88% sell-side lit flow reinforces supply.

**Dealer gamma / timing context:** Data unavailable — verify in R2. Event-driven flow on 3/11 (circuit breaker session) heavily inflated single-session metrics. Formal catalyst just arrived today, creating potential "sell the news" risk.

**Downgrade factors:**
- Net unwind across contract OI (0/6 rising)
- 3/6 contracts bid-heavy; 59% bid-side multi-day alerts
- $20 call OI peaked 3/11 and declining (closing flow)
- Stock below primary dark-pool cluster level ($17.45)
- Lit flow 88% sell-side
- 2/4 detailed contracts are 0DTE noise
- Elevated IV across all lines without sufficient OI build
- Volume/price divergence on 3/12
- "Buy rumor / sell news" dynamic risk with directive now formally announced
- Material litigation overhang from state AG and DA criminal charges

---

## **[NEUTRAL / MIXED]**

The catalyst is genuine, material, and now formally confirmed — the DPA directive is a major positive fundamental development. However, the **tape does not confirm sustained bullish positioning.** The options flow is overwhelmingly a story of event-driven spike activity on 3/11 followed by profit-taking and OI decline. Only one contract line ($15 ITM call) shows a credible multi-session build. Dark-pool structure shows the stock failing to hold above the key $17.45 level. Distribution signatures (bid-heavy options, sell-side lit flow) dominate the most recent sessions. The tape looks like the market front-ran the directive on the Bloomberg rumor (3/11) and is now digesting/distributing rather than building fresh conviction. If the stock reclaims and holds above $17.45 with fresh OI build on the 3/20 expiry calls in the next 1–2 sessions, the read could be upgraded. As of the current data snapshot, the tape is mixed.

The 112% month-to-date rally demonstrates the market has already repriced the federal intervention. The active California
confirmation_details.raw_output:
CATALYST CONFIRMATION
Ticker: SOC
Direction: Bullish
Primary catalyst: Trump administration federal directive under Defense Production Act to restore the Santa Ynez oil unit, overriding California state regulatory blockade
Catalyst profile: Idiosyncratic regulatory unlock compounded by concurrent global oil supply shock from Iran war / Strait of Hormuz disruption
Catalyst proxy basket: SOC direct, crude oil (WTI/Brent), California offshore E&P regulatory pathway, DPA legal precedent
Dim 1 Score: 35
Dim 2 Score: N/A — not provided
Dim 3 Score: 82
Weighting basis: Dim 2 unavailable. Reweighted across Dim 1 (60%) and Dim 3 (40%). Time-to-resolution is medium-speed (1–3 weeks for repricing, months for full legal resolution), so options weighted more heavily given no prediction market data.
Primary CDS: 54
Disagreement flag: MODERATE DISAGREEMENT
Collision check: California AG Rob Bonta has filed suit against the Trump administration to block any DPA-driven restart. The move has triggered immediate pushback from California. Attorney General Rob Bonta has filed suit against the Trump administration to block any DPA-driven restart, reinforcing the state's position that Sable's infrastructure remains too risky. California's Attorney General has already vowed to challenge the DOJ's OLC opinion in the Supreme Court, citing the Tenth Amendment. This is the core identified competing catalyst. Opposition Weight: 0.30 — the lawsuit is a direct constitutional challenge to the primary catalyst mechanism. Competing CDS estimate: 50 (credible legal challenge, active litigation, but federal preemption doctrine and DPA framework favor the administration in the near term). Effective CDS = 54 − (50 × 0.30) = 39.
Effective CDS: 39
Catalyst health state: COLLIDING

Dimension 1 — Options / positioning:
The provided flow data is materially negative for catalyst confirmation. The overall contract picture shows net unwind with 0/6 contracts exhibiting rising OI. The largest OI builds at the $20 strike peaked on 3/11 and have been declining — consistent with profit-taking, not fresh conviction into the catalyst. 59% bid-side across multi-day alerts signals distribution. Lit flow is 88% sell-side. The stock closed at $17.45 on 3/12, sitting right at the primary dark-pool cluster level, not above it. Two of four detailed contracts are 0DTE with negligible remaining signal value. Elevated IV across the chain reflects event premium, not directional conviction build. Despite the tape read being labeled "supportive," the underlying granular evidence — net OI unwind, profit-taking on the largest positions, dominant sell-side lit flow, and bid-heavy distribution — paints a picture of smart money distributing into the catalyst headline rather than building fresh conviction. Score: 35. This is a material negative for catalyst health confirmation.

Dimension 2 — Prediction markets:
No prediction-market data was provided. Cannot score. Omitted from composite.

Dimension 3 — Cross-asset:
The cross-asset complex strongly confirms the commodity tailwind component of the thesis. Brent crude oil closed above $100 on Friday for the second consecutive session, as the Iran war heads toward its third week. Brent futures rose 2.67% to close at $103.14 per barrel. U.S. crude oil gained 3.11% to settle at $98.71 per barrel. Oil prices have notched another week of gains, with Brent futures up about 10%. It follows the 27.9% rise seen last week, which marked the biggest weekly gain in oil prices since the Covid-19 pandemic in 2020. The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively blockaded. Overall shipments in the strait have all but halted, according to IEA executive director Faithe Birol. On March 14, Kharg Island became the latest epicentre of the US-Israel war on Iran, after the U.S. air force bombed military facilities on the Iranian island. The oil supply shock is active, escalating, and directly supportive of SOC's commodity exposure. The federal directive itself was confirmed hours ago by CNBC: the Trump administration on Friday directed Sable Offshore to restore oil drilling operations off the southern California coast, the U.S. Energy Department said. U.S. Secretary of Energy Chris Wright told Sable to restart work at the Santa Ynez unit and Santa Ynez pipeline "to address supply disruption risks caused by California policies." The federal government issued the order under the authority of the Defense Production Act of 1950. The DOJ legal opinion backing this is robust — "We conclude that it would," the opinion states in response to whether a DPA order could override California laws blocking pipeline operations. A presidential order under the DPA could override certain state restrictions and could shield companies from penalties tied to state laws if those laws conflict with compliance under the federal directive. Cross-asset alignment is strong: oil surging, federal action confirmed, DPA legal framework articulated. Score: 82. However, the stock has already rallied massively — the stock has surged 112%, climbing from approximately $12.00 to over $25.00 in less than two weeks — meaning the "lag" the thesis depends on has partially closed already. The stock then pulled back sharply on the California lawsuit and Hunterbrook report: SOC shares tumbled nearly 16% to $10.43 on Friday after the latest Hunterbrook report and news of California's lawsuit. It then recovered to the $17 area, suggesting the market is deeply conflicted.

Why this matters for the trade:
The primary catalyst — the federal DPA directive — has officially been issued as of today (3/14). This is a genuine regulatory silver bullet and represents the strongest possible federal action short of a presidential executive order. The oil macro tailwind is among the most extreme in recent history with Brent above $100 and the Strait of Hormuz blockaded. However, three critical factors degrade catalyst health: (1) Options positioning shows net unwind and distribution, not fresh conviction build — smart money appears to be selling into the headline. (2) California's AG has filed suit to block the DPA restart, setting up a direct confrontation between Washington and Sacramento over who controls the project's fate. Judge Donna Geck upheld the restraining order blocking the company from restarting production. The state-level injunction remains in place. The DPA directive may override it, but that will be litigated — SYU watchers see this State-Federal battle ultimately ending up in the Supreme Court. (3) The stock has already repriced significantly from ~$12 to ~$17–25 range in two weeks, meaning a substantial portion of the "mispricing" has already been captured or is now a two-way bet on the legal outcome rather than a clean informational advantage.

Recommended action: DOWNGRADE

Primary downgrade / kill reason:
Options positioning is in net unwind and distribution mode across the entire contract structure, directly contradicting the thesis of "under-appreciated catalyst being ignored." The 112% month-to-date rally demonstrates the market has already repriced the federal intervention. The active California AG lawsuit against the DPA order creates a binary legal collision that degrades the thesis from "regulatory unlock" to "constitutional showdown with uncertain outcome." The Effective CDS of 39 places this squarely in the DOWNGRADE band. The positioning dimension (Dim 1) and the legal collision together override what would otherwise be a strong cross-asset read.

Key missing checks for R2:
— Prediction-market pricing on California injunction success / DPA legal challenge outcome
— Fresh options flow data on Monday 3/16 post-directive to see if new conviction builds or distribution accelerates
— Dark-pool activity in SOC specifically post-directive (current data is pre-directive, as of 3/13)
— California AG's specific legal filing and any emergency TRO request timeline
— Judge Geck's calendar and next hearing date on the existing state injunction
— Whether PHMSA or DOE issues implementation guidance that directly supersedes the state court injunction
— SOC's response or 8-K filing in response to the DPA directive
— Updated short interest data
— Whether the $17.45 dark-pool cluster level acts as support or resistance on the first post-directive trading session

survivors.catalyst: U.S. bombing of Kharg Island military targets Friday evening, with Trump threatening to strike oil infrastructure if Iran continues blocking the Strait of Hormuz. Millions of barrels of oil remain trapped in the Persian Gulf with Strait traffic effectively at a standstill, and Iran's new supreme leader vowed to keep the Strait closed. Jet fuel prices have doubled since the conflict, far exceeding the roughly one-third rise in crude oil prices.
survivors.catalyst_event_date: Ongoing since Feb 28; Kharg Island strike announced post-market Mar 13
survivors.market_visibility_date: Kharg Island bombing — Friday evening Mar 13 (after market close); crack spread blowout — Mar 4-12 via Reuters
survivors.source_quality: Mixed (primary government/IEA sources for oil and conflict data; secondary reporting for airline-specific financials)
survivors.cleanest_expression: United Airlines and Carnival Corp entered 2026 with 0% of their fuel requirements hedged, leaving them completely exposed. United Airlines has disclosed that every $1 change in the price of a barrel of fuel impacts its annual expenses by approximately $116 million. AAL shares this 0% hedging posture. An analyst noted downside risk to estimates as higher jet fuel prices are incorporated, stating "we see most risk for American and now forecast negative EPS this year," downgrading AAL from buy to neutral — and that call was made at ~$77 oil, not today's ~$99. AAL's weaker balance sheet versus peers creates maximum operating leverage to sustained fuel cost increases. The widening gap between crude oil and refined jet fuel costs has created intense pressure on airline margins, and most hedging programs are tied to crude oil benchmarks rather than the refined fuel airlines actually consume. Even partially hedged peers are exposed; AAL's 0% hedging makes it the purest bearish expression.
survivors.what_moved: The price of Brent crude has gained more than 40 percent since the war began in late February. Jet fuel prices have doubled since the Iran conflict, far outpacing a one-third rise in crude prices. In Asia, jet fuel refining margins widened from about $21/bbl pre-conflict to as much as $144, remaining unusually high at $65 as of Wednesday.
survivors.what_repriced: (and approximate magnitude): American Airlines and JetBlue saw double-digit percentage drops as their unhedged positions left them equally exposed to the spot market. UAL's share price cratered by 33% on March 12 alone. AAL has experienced comparable severe selling since late February.
survivors.what_lagged: (1) The Kharg Island bombing was announced Friday evening after market close — Trump on Friday evening announced he'd ordered strikes on Kharg Island, which is an unpriced Monday gap event. (2) The analyst downgrade to negative EPS was at ~$77 oil; at $99 WTI the negative EPS is materially worse and consensus has not caught up. (3) The mismatch between crude oil hedging and jet fuel prices becomes particularly problematic during geopolitical crises that disrupt refining capacity. AAL with 0% hedging of any kind faces the full brunt of the crack spread blowout — an incremental headwind beyond crude-focused models. (4) Prediction markets assign only a 19.5% probability to the Iran conflict resolving by March 31, suggesting duration risk is higher than many models assume.
survivors.payoff_range: : 8–15% additional downside over 1–2 weeks, assuming oil sustains above $95 and no ceasefire
survivors.thesis: AAL is the most fuel-exposed major U.S. airline — 0% hedged with the weakest balance sheet among the big three. The Friday evening Kharg Island escalation creates an unpriced Monday catalyst. Jet fuel doubling versus crude's ~40% rise means AAL's true cost hit is far worse than headline oil suggests, and the Street's downward earnings revision cycle — initiated at $77 oil — has not caught up to $99+ reality.
survivors.transmission_type: First-order (oil/jet fuel → airline fuel cost → EPS destruction). The primary lag is the post-close Kharg Island escalation creating an immediate Monday repricing event. The secondary lag is that sell-side consensus has not yet revised to sustained $99+ oil, and the crack spread amplification is structurally undermodeled in most airline coverage.
survivors.why_late: An analyst already forecast negative EPS when oil was at ~$77. Oil is now $99 with Brent at $103. The earnings impact at current levels is roughly 30% worse than the initial downgrade scenario, yet consensus has not been fully revised. The crack spread angle means the true fuel cost is even worse than the crude-implied estimate. The post-close Kharg Island escalation has not been priced.
survivors.already_priced_risk: Airline stocks have already been decimated — UAL down 33% in a single day, AAL in double-digit decline territory. Peak panic may already be reflected. White House officials have said they expect oil prices to fall dramatically once Operation Epic Fury comes to an end. Any ceasefire signal or Strait reopening would trigger a violent short squeeze. The 400-million-barrel IEA reserve release and U.S. easing of Russian oil sanctions are designed to cap prices.
survivors.evidence: (1) Kharg Island bombing post-close Friday — not yet reflected in AAL stock. (2) Analyst negative EPS forecast at $77 oil vs. current $99 — material revision gap. (3) Jet fuel doubled vs. crude up ~40% — incremental damage beyond crude-focused models. (4) Sub-20% conflict resolution probability by March 31 implies duration risk the market may underweight.
survivors.extension_read: Stock already down heavily from pre-war levels. However, the Kharg escalation is a fresh catalyst with no prior pricing, and the crack spread dimension adds an underappreciated layer. The bear thesis is partially priced but not exhausted given continued war escalation and low resolution probability.
survivors.historical_check: During the 2022 Russia-Ukraine oil spike (Brent above $120), U.S. airline stocks experienced 30–50% drawdowns over several weeks. The current situation is arguably more severe for unhedged carriers because of the unprecedented crack spread blowout and 0% hedging posture. Historically, fuel price spikes during Middle Eastern conflicts have remained elevated for months.
survivors.practical_tradability: Adequate (AAL is liquid with high daily volume; however, borrow availability for short positions and extreme IV on puts must be verified in R2)
survivors.actionable_now: The Kharg Island post-close escalation creates a specific Monday repricing catalyst. Oil above $100, crack spread at extreme levels, 0% hedging, and sub-20% resolution probability by month-end collectively argue the bearish revision cycle continues.
survivors.invalidation: Ceasefire or Strait reopening announcement over the weekend. Oil falling below $85 on SPR release, diplomatic breakthrough, or Russian supply normalization. Government airline support or bailout intervention.
survivors.crowding_risk: Medium (airline-bearish trade is a common expression of the oil shock, but the crack spread amplification angle and post-close Kharg timing provide differentiation; the trade is not as crowded as the direct oil-long expression)
survivors.confidence: Low (significant repricing has already occurred; the remaining gap depends on duration risk and Monday-specific gap from Kharg Island escalation; elevated IV may limit practical returns on options)
survivors.r2_uncertainties: Exact AAL stock decline from pre-war levels to quantify remaining gap. Current borrow availability and short interest. Implied volatility levels on puts and put spread pricing. Q1 2026 earnings date. Updated sell-side consensus EPS at current oil levels. Whether AAL has added any hedging since the war began. Balance sheet liquidity runway at $99+ oil for extended period.
survivors.idiosyncratic_risks: AAL's weaker balance sheet creates both opportunity (maximum earnings leverage) and unpredictable risk (potential credit events, government intervention, restructuring speculation). Binary ceasefire risk could cause violent squeeze. Extremely elevated IV makes options expensive and may erode returns even on a correct directional call.
survivors.source_caveat: AAL's 0% hedging posture and the negative EPS forecast are sourced from secondary financial reporting citing analyst notes and regulatory filings. Kharg Island timing confirmed from multiple primary sources (White House, CNBC, Foreign Policy, Axios). Crack spread data from Reuters/BofA. Some specific AAL figures require verification against primary company filings in R2.

survivors.catalyst: Trump administration federal directive to restore the Santa Ynez oil unit.
survivors.cleanest_expression: SOC is effectively a single-asset entity formed specifically to acquire and restart the Santa Ynez Unit (SYU) offshore California. The federal directive directly targets their sole operational bottleneck and existential regulatory overhang.
survivors.what_moved: Global crude oil has spiked violently (driven by the US bombing of Kharg Island and Strait of Hormuz threats), and the broader equity market has liquidated (S&P 500 hitting new lows for the year).
survivors.what_repriced: (and approximate magnitude): Data unavailable — verify in R2
survivors.what_lagged: The compounding valuation effect of a massive idiosyncratic regulatory unlock occurring simultaneously with a generational spike in the underlying commodity (oil), which is likely being masked by broad index liquidation.
survivors.thesis: The Trump administration has officially directed Sable Offshore to restore the Santa Ynez oil unit, directly neutralizing the company's primary regulatory overhang. This idiosyncratic silver-bullet catalyst is hitting the tape on the exact same day that global oil prices are surging due to US strikes on Iran's Kharg Island. Because the broader equity market is in a severe liquidation phase, SOC's massive fundamental unlock is highly likely to be lost in the noise or sold down with the market, creating a severely mispriced entry for a company that just won its regulatory battle alongside a massive commodity tailwind.
survivors.transmission_type: First-Order. Lag exists because the news is breaking during a severe macro liquidation event, causing idiosyncratic positive news to be overshadowed by broad de-risking, index selling, and geopolitical panic.
survivors.why_late: Retail and institutional bandwidth is entirely consumed by the Iran war, Strait of Hormuz threats, and the resulting broad market selloff.
survivors.already_priced_risk: The market may have aggressively bid SOC up in the pre-market immediately upon the headline crossing, or investors may heavily discount the federal directive's legal ability to quickly override California state law.
survivors.evidence: 1) The catalyst dropped on a Friday amidst a headline-dominating macro panic and S&P 500 new lows, a classic structural environment for idiosyncratic news to be ignored or sold into by index funds. 2) The simultaneous spike in oil prices provides a secondary valuation tailwind that compounds the regulatory win, a dual-factor shock that is rarely priced efficiently in a single panic-driven session.
survivors.actionable_now: The convergence of a binary regulatory win and a massive macro commodity tailwind during a broad market panic creates a rare dislocation and entry opportunity.
survivors.invalidation: California state regulators immediately file an injunction that successfully blocks the federal directive, trapping SOC in prolonged litigation despite the administration's order.
survivors.r2_uncertainties: Exact intraday price response of SOC to the headline; legal viability and timeline of the federal directive vs. California Coastal Commission jurisdiction.
survivors.idiosyncratic_risks: Single-asset concentration risk; heavy debt load typical of SPAC E&P acquisitions; state vs. federal legal jurisdiction battles.
survivors.source_caveat: Relies on CNBC headline "Trump administration directs Sable Offshore to restore Santa Ynez oil unit."
